campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall spending. They also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, such as adding bike lanes, better bus routes, or pedestrian pathways.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and city money management
City spending and budget report for July
New transportation improvements (biking, walking, public transit options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could comment on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced an ordinance (a new city law) and decided to skip the usual first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance will come back for a final vote at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather community input
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for adoption vote at next meeting
Fast-tracked approval process used

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and to authorize the Town Manager to enter into a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused on routine administrative approvals and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approval of multiple sets of March meeting minutes
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Administrative housekeeping from previous closed and open sessions

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 14, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to handle several routine administrative and infrastructure matters. The main focus was approving changes to engineering contracts for wastewater and water recycling projects, along with standard items like council member absences and board appointments.
keyTopics: Changes to engineering contracts with HDR Engineering and CDM Smith for wastewater facility work
Budget increase for the South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 project
Updates to habitat conservation agreements
Council member absences and board/commission appointments

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026, where they reviewed the city's spending plans for upcoming building and infrastructure projects. The council also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to celebrate young people and community volunteers in the city.
keyTopics: 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program (city construction and infrastructure projects)
Teen Month Proclamation
National Volunteer Week Proclamation

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met to review their previous meeting notes and introduced a new proposal to update tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The changes aim to strengthen housing protections for residents renting in the city.
keyTopics: Approval of March 25, 2026 meeting minutes
New updates to tenant protection laws (Chapter 19.71 of city code)
Residential renter safeguards and rights

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026, to help residents understand and use OpenGov, a tool that shows where the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules about who has to register if they're trying to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool demo and community training
City budget spending visibility for residents
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city law
How to participate in city budget decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026, using both in-person and online options so residents could participate however works best for them. The council is making it easy for people to join and share their thoughts, whether they show up in person or join remotely through Zoom.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format - attend in person or via Zoom
Remote participation options for residents who can't attend in person
Written public comment submission process available online
Meeting accessibility through phone call-in option

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle several administrative matters, including updating the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030, reviewing new project requests from individual council members, and considering changes to how council vacancies are filled. The meeting also covered standard items like approving previous meeting minutes and planning the calendar for upcoming council meetings.
keyTopics: Updates to federal funding and housing plan for 2025-2030
New project requests from individual council members
Changes to the council vacancy process
Approval of previous meeting minutes and next meeting agenda preview

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto. The owner is asking to officially recognize the building as more historically important, which would allow them to add 5,000 square feet of extra floor space if they fix up the building and make it safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic status upgrade from Category 4 to Category 2
5,000 sq ft bonus floor area for seismic and historic rehabilitation work
Downtown Commercial zone development with pedestrian shopping considerations
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
